frameworks.name: 10000-year-clock
frameworks.description: Think at civilization-scale timeframes by asking how decisions look over 10,000 years, countering short-term pathological thinking

frameworks.name: availability-heuristic
frameworks.description: Correct probability and frequency judgments distorted by mentally available examples by seeking base rate data before relying on vivid anecdotes

frameworks.name: catalysts
frameworks.description: Identify and deploy small interventions that lower activation energy for desired change without being consumed - accelerate reactions that would happen anyway

frameworks.name: cathedral-thinking
frameworks.description: Plan and execute multi-generational projects you won't live to see completed, laboring in the present for future glory and legacy

frameworks.name: circle-of-competence
frameworks.description: Know the boundaries of your expertise and operate within them - success comes from playing where you have an edge, not from expanding into unfamiliar territory

frameworks.name: cold-start-problem
frameworks.description: Five-stage framework for launching and scaling network effect products from zero to defensible moat

frameworks.name: confirmation-bias
frameworks.description: Combat the tendency to seek evidence supporting existing beliefs by actively searching for disconfirming data and treating opinions as testable hypotheses

frameworks.name: daci-framework
frameworks.description: Decision-making framework clarifying who drives, approves, contributes to, and gets informed about decisions

frameworks.name: delphi-method
frameworks.description: Build consensus expert forecasts through iterative anonymous surveys with controlled feedback to minimize groupthink and anchor biases

frameworks.name: design-of-everyday-things
frameworks.description: Apply Don Norman's affordances, signifiers, feedback, and conceptual models to make interfaces discoverable and learnable without instruction

frameworks.name: design-systems
frameworks.description: Create single source of truth for product design through reusable components, design tokens, guidelines, and documentation ensuring consistency at scale

frameworks.name: dichotomy-of-control
frameworks.description: Distinguish what you control (judgments, actions) from what you don't (externals, outcomes) to focus energy wisely and achieve tranquility

frameworks.name: domain-driven-design
frameworks.description: Model complex software to match the problem domain by building ubiquitous language with domain experts and organizing code into bounded contexts

frameworks.name: dont-make-me-think
frameworks.description: Web usability principles focused on intuitive, self-evident design that requires minimal cognitive effort

frameworks.name: dry-principle
frameworks.description: Eliminate code duplication by extracting shared logic into single authoritative representations - every piece of knowledge should exist once

frameworks.name: dynamic-programming
frameworks.description: Solve optimization problems by breaking them into overlapping subproblems, caching solutions to avoid redundant computation

frameworks.name: feedback-loops
frameworks.description: Identify reinforcing loops that drive exponential growth and balancing loops that resist change - system behavior emerges from which loop dominates

frameworks.name: first-principles-thinking
frameworks.description: Break down complex problems to fundamental truths and rebuild solutions from scratch when existing approaches are too expensive or inefficient

frameworks.name: fishbone-diagram
frameworks.description: Visually map all potential causes of a problem across structured categories to systematically identify root causes through team collaboration

frameworks.name: five-whys
frameworks.description: Uncover root causes by iteratively asking why a problem occurred, moving from symptoms to underlying system failures

frameworks.name: fmea
frameworks.description: Systematically identify potential failure modes, assess their severity and likelihood, and prioritize preventive actions using risk priority numbers

frameworks.name: fog-of-war
frameworks.description: Make decisions under uncertainty by acknowledging incomplete information, gathering intelligence, and adapting as situation clarity improves

frameworks.name: force-multipliers
frameworks.description: Identify tools, tactics, or resources that amplify effectiveness disproportionately to cost, enabling smaller forces to defeat larger ones

frameworks.name: growth-loops
frameworks.description: Build self-reinforcing growth systems where user actions generate inputs that acquire more users, compounding over time

frameworks.name: high-output-management
frameworks.description: Output-oriented management framework focused on leverage, measurement, and systematic team performance multiplication

frameworks.name: hooked-model
frameworks.description: Build habit-forming products using Nir Eyal's Trigger-Action-Variable Reward-Investment cycle to increase engagement and retention

frameworks.name: information-architecture
frameworks.description: Organize content and navigation structures so users can find information intuitively using card sorting, tree testing, and hierarchy design

frameworks.name: inversion
frameworks.description: Solve problems by identifying what would cause failure instead of optimizing for success - reveals critical failure modes that forward thinking obscures

frameworks.name: jobs-to-be-done
frameworks.description: Understand customer motivations by uncovering the job they hire your product to do, not demographics or features they request

frameworks.name: mom-test
frameworks.description: Customer interview methodology that extracts truthful insights by focusing on past behavior over future intentions

frameworks.name: mungers-latticework
frameworks.description: Build an interconnected network of mental models from multiple disciplines to analyze problems through diverse lenses and avoid single-perspective blind spots

frameworks.name: network-effects
frameworks.description: Leverage network effects where product value increases as more users join, creating winner-take-all dynamics and defensible moats

frameworks.name: nielsen-heuristics
frameworks.description: 10 foundational usability principles for evaluating and designing user interfaces

frameworks.name: north-star-metric
frameworks.description: Single metric framework that captures core product value and aligns teams on sustainable growth

frameworks.name: okrs
frameworks.description: Set ambitious goals using Objectives (qualitative direction) and Key Results (quantitative milestones) to align teams and measure progress

frameworks.name: ooda-loop
frameworks.description: Outmaneuver competition by cycling through observe-orient-decide-act faster than opponents, operating inside their decision-making tempo

frameworks.name: prompt-engineering
frameworks.description: Systematic techniques for structuring LLM instructions to produce accurate, consistent, and high-quality outputs

frameworks.name: prospect-theory
frameworks.description: Understand how people evaluate risk irrationally through loss aversion and reference points - predict decisions by recognizing pain of loss outweighs equivalent gain

frameworks.name: rag-patterns
frameworks.description: Retrieval-Augmented Generation architectures that enhance LLMs with real-time external knowledge to reduce hallucinations

frameworks.name: red-queen-effect
frameworks.description: Recognize competitive environments where you must continuously evolve just to maintain position - standing still means falling behind

frameworks.name: rice-prioritization
frameworks.description: Scoring framework using Reach, Impact, Confidence, and Effort to objectively prioritize product features and projects

frameworks.name: second-order-thinking
frameworks.description: Think beyond immediate consequences to understand cascading effects and long-term impacts. Use when making high-stakes decisions, evaluating long-term strategies, or when everyone agrees on the obvious solution. Apply systematic "and then what?" questioning to trace causality chains through time and interconnected systems.

frameworks.name: shell-method-scenario-planning
frameworks.description: Navigate strategic uncertainty through multiple plausible future scenarios rather than single-point forecasting. Developed at Royal Dutch Shell, this framework replaces single-point predictions with 2-4 rich narratives representing genuinely different futures defined by critical uncertainties.

frameworks.name: solid-principles
frameworks.description: Write maintainable object-oriented code by following five design principles - Single Responsibility, Open-Closed, Liskov Substitution, Interface Segregation, Dependency Inversion

frameworks.name: superforecasting
frameworks.description: Make systematically accurate predictions by breaking problems into sub-questions, using base rates, and updating beliefs incrementally with Brier score tracking

frameworks.name: supply-and-demand
frameworks.description: Predict price and quantity changes by analyzing how supply (what producers offer) and demand (what consumers want) interact at market equilibrium

frameworks.name: system-1-system-2
frameworks.description: Recognize when you're using fast intuitive thinking versus slow deliberate reasoning - match the thinking mode to the decision's complexity and stakes

frameworks.name: system-archetypes
frameworks.description: Recognize recurring system behavior patterns like limits-to-growth and tragedy-of-commons to predict failure modes and design interventions

frameworks.name: tetlocks-10-commandments
frameworks.description: Improve forecast accuracy by following Philip Tetlock's 10 evidence-based principles from superforecaster research distinguishing top 2% predictors

frameworks.name: thinking-in-bets
frameworks.description: Frame decisions as probability bets instead of certainties - separate decision quality from outcome quality to avoid resulting bias

frameworks.name: triz-40-principles
frameworks.description: Resolve engineering contradictions by applying one of 40 inventive principles systematically derived from 200,000 patents instead of trial-and-error

frameworks.name: twelve-leverage-points
frameworks.description: Find the highest-impact intervention points in a complex system - changing paradigms beats tweaking parameters by 1000x

frameworks.name: user-research-methodologies
frameworks.description: Systematically uncover user needs, behaviors, and pain points through interviews, usability tests, surveys, and ethnographic observation

frameworks.name: wcag
frameworks.description: Make web content accessible to people with disabilities by following POUR principles - Perceivable, Operable, Understandable, Robust
